generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize repairs that address unresolved identifiers or invalid type declarations.
Ensure AGREE contracts reference only visible AADL features or declared helpers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `int`, `real`, and `bool` for AGREE scalar type declarations.
Guarantees should relate visible outputs to visible inputs.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing unqualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not place implementation-level bindings within component type annexes.
Do not attempt to repair by adding new AADL architecture; focus on fixing existing AGREE expressions.
